fund.name: HDFC Flexi Cap Fund - Growth Option - Direct Plan

allocations.rationale: Anchor fund with 31-year track record, ₹32,020 Cr AUM, and consistent 20.5% 5-year return. Low tracking error (3.88%) ensures genuine active management. Equity-taxed, suitable for 30% slab.

fund.name: BANK OF INDIA Flexi Cap Fund Direct Plan -Growth
fund.fund_house: Bank of India Investment Managers Private Limited

allocations.rationale: High-alpha (18.3) flexi-cap with best-in-class Sharpe (1.10) and information ratio (1.88). 23.6% 3-year CAGR justifies active management at 1% fee. Equity-taxed, avoids slab taxation.

fund.name: Nippon India Growth Mid Cap Fund - Direct Plan Growth Plan - Growth Option
fund.fund_house: Nippon Life India Asset Management Limited

allocations.rationale: Top-tier mid cap with 26.6% 3-year CAGR, 13.7 alpha, and 1.12 Sharpe ratio. 30-year legacy (₹8,172 Cr AUM) provides stability in volatile segment. Equity-taxed.

fund.name: BANDHAN SMALL CAP FUND - DIRECT PLAN GROWTH

allocations.rationale: Highest 3-year CAGR (31.3%) among small caps with ₹8,900 Cr AUM. Alpha (9.8) and consistency justify 1.2% fee. Equity-taxed for tax efficiency in 30% slab.

fund.name: Motilal Oswal Nifty Midcap 150 Index Fund - Direct Plan
fund.fund_house: Motilal Oswal Asset Management Company Limited

allocations.rationale: Low-cost (0.2% fee) index exposure with 11.7 alpha — outperforming benchmark due to smart rebalancing. 23.2% 3-year CAGR, 1.00 Sharpe. Equity-taxed.

fund.name: ICICI Prudential Equity & Debt Fund - Direct Plan - Growth
fund.fund_house: ICICI Prudential Asset Management Company Limited

allocations.rationale: Stabilizer with 9.3 alpha and 1.49 information ratio. 65%+ equity ensures equity taxation (12.5% LTCG) vs. slab-taxing debt funds. Reduces portfolio volatility.

fund.name: Sundaram Long Term Tax Advantage Fund Series III Direct Plan - Growth

allocations.rationale: Tax alpha play: ₹1.5L annual 80C deduction saves ₹46.8k/year at 30% slab. 18.6 alpha and 21.8% 5-year CAGR justify 1.05% fee. 3-year lock-in accepted for tax arbitrage.

plan.setup_phase:
Step 1: Open a direct mutual fund account on Groww or Kuvera.
Step 2: Complete KYC using your PAN and Aadhaar.
Step 3: Link your HDFC Bank account (preferred for zero interbank charges).
Step 4: Start SIPs immediately for all 7 funds with auto-debit enabled.
Step 5: Allocate lumpsum ₹40 Lakh across funds using same 25:20:15:15:10:10:5 ratio.
Step 6: Enable email/SMS alerts for SIP execution and NAV updates.
review_checkpoints: After 6 months: Verify all SIPs executed. Check if any fund underperformed category average by >2% in 6M CAGR.
After 1 year: Compare each fund's 1-year return vs. category median. Replace any fund lagging by >3% with higher-alpha peer.
After 3 years: Rebalance portfolio if allocation deviates by >5% from target due to differential growth.
plan.rebalancing_guidelines: Rebalance annually. If any category (e.g., small cap) grows beyond ±5% of target allocation, book profits and redeploy to underweight categories. Use only capital gains for rebalancing to avoid tax.
plan.rationale: At 34 with 18-year horizon, portfolio maximizes equity exposure (95%) while optimizing for Sharpe ratio. Flexi-cap (45%) provides core stability with alpha generation. Mid/small caps (30%) drive growth. Index fund (10%) ensures low-cost market capture. Aggressive hybrid (10%) reduces volatility. ELSS (5%) delivers tax alpha. All funds equity-taxed to avoid 30% slab taxation on gains. ₹1.5L/year ELSS SIP fully utilizes 80C, saving ₹46.8k/year in taxes — this reinvested savings compound to ₹28.5L over 18 years at 10% return.
risks: Stock markets can drop 30-40% in a bad year — your portfolio value will fall temporarily. This is normal.
Small and mid cap funds are volatile — can fall 50% in crashes but recover over 3-5 years.
ELSS has 3-year lock-in — money can't be withdrawn early.
Past returns don't guarantee future results — high-alpha funds may underperform if market regime changes.
Concentration risk: 45% in flexi-cap means performance heavily depends on these two funds.
plan.disclaimer: For research/educational purposes only. Not certified financial advice.

sip_step_up.description: Increase SIP by 10% every year to match salary growth and inflation

aps.reasoning:
passive_instrument_fraction: Only 10% of the equity allocation (Motilal Oswal Nifty Midcap 150 Index Fund) is in a passive/index instrument; the remaining 90% is in actively managed flexi, mid, and small cap funds. Thus, the score is low (0.1).

turnover_score: The plan includes annual rebalancing and explicit review checkpoints at 6 months, 1 year, and 3 years with potential fund replacements based on performance gaps. This indicates active management and frequent oversight, resulting in a low score (0.2).

cost_emphasis_score: Expense ratios are acknowledged but justified by high alpha and performance (e.g., 1.2% fee for Bandhan Small Cap justified by 31.3% CAGR). Cost minimisation is not a primary criterion; performance and tax efficiency dominate. Hence, score is low (0.1).

research_vs_cost_score: Fund selection is based on deep performance metrics (alpha, Sharpe, information ratio), 30-year legacy, and fund-specific advantages — indicating strong emphasis on research and active management. No indication of broad market exposure without analysis. Score is 0.0.

time_horizon_alignment_score: The investor is 34 with an 18-year horizon, and the plan includes long-term allocation shifts (at year 10 and 15). Despite active reviews, the core strategy is long-term compounding. This earns a high score (0.8).

portfolio_activeness_score: The portfolio is dominated by high-alpha, high-information-ratio funds (e.g., alpha > 9 in most, IR > 1.3). Even the index fund shows high tracking error (8.79%) and alpha (11.7), suggesting active deviations. Debt/hybrid component (ICICI Equity & Debt) is actively managed. Overall, this is a genuinely active portfolio. Score is low (0.1).

pqs.reasoning: The investment plan is highly aligned with Sheldon Cooper's aggressive risk appetite, 18-year horizon, and goal of retirement with a ₹12Cr portfolio. The 95% equity allocation, inclusion of high-growth mid/small caps, and step-down allocation over time reflect a sophisticated, goal-anchored strategy. Diversification is strong across market caps (flexi, mid, small), fund houses, and styles (active and index), though 45% in flexi-cap introduces mild concentration risk. Risk-return is appropriate: volatility is acknowledged, rebalancing rules are clear, and projected returns (19.1% base) are ambitious but plausible given historical fund performance. The plan is internally consistent: rationale, allocations, rebalancing, and risk warnings cohere logically. Tax efficiency is optimal: the investor is in the 30% slab, and the plan correctly uses equity-taxed funds (including Aggressive Hybrid and ELSS) to avoid slab taxation on debt. ELSS is included to fully utilize 80C benefits, saving ₹46.8k/year in taxes—a clear tax alpha. All funds are equity-taxed, and the rationale explicitly justifies this for tax efficiency. No slab-taxed debt funds are used unnecessarily. Thus, tax_efficiency earns a perfect score.
